identity.name: tg_bot_history (Module)
identity.purpose: Object-oriented Python module for monitoring, parsing, and persisting Telegram interaction data into a structured SQLite database.

components.class: 1. Encapsulates SQLite operations and connection management. 2. Implements idempotent UPSERT patterns for all primary entities. 3. Manages database initialization and PRAGMA settings (WAL, Foreign Keys). 4. Provides high-level aggregation methods for reporting and exporting.

1. Primary interface for the application layer for data ingestion. 2. Receives an instance of DatabaseRepository via constructor (Dependency Injection). 3. Contains private methods for extracting metadata from aiogram objects. 4. Orchestrates the flow: Extract -> Transform -> Persist.

table.name: users
table.description: Unique Telegram users with profile metadata.
table.fields: id (PK), username, first_name, last_name, is_bot, language_code, updated_at.

table.name: chats
table.description: Telegram groups, supergroups, channels, and private chats.
table.fields: id (PK), type, title, username, description, updated_at.

table.name: messages
table.description: Log of captured messages with edit history support. Uses Telegram-native IDs as keys.
table.fields: message_id (PK), chat_id (PK), sender_id, reply_to_message_id, quote_text, quote_entities, quote_offset, quote_is_manual, forward_sender_id, forward_message_id, forward_sender_name, original_text, text, entities (JSON), media_group_id, date, edit_date.

table.name: message_media
table.description: Metadata for files attached to messages. Linked via composite key.
table.fields: message_id (PK/FK), chat_id (PK/FK), file_unique_id (PK), file_id, file_type, file_size, mime_type, width, height.

table.name: chat_members
table.description: Intersection table for user status and activity within specific chats.
table.fields: chat_id (PK), user_id (PK), status, joined_at, left_at, first_activity, last_activity, updated_at.

principles.principle: Stateful Interaction: Dependency-based instantiation of the HistoryCollector class.
Encapsulation: DatabaseRepository handles its own connection lifecycle per method call.
Agnosticism: The module remains independent of the bot's access control lists (ACLs).

integration_logic.initialization_flow: 1. Instantiate repo = DatabaseRepository(db_path). 2. Call repo.init_db() to ensure schema integrity and apply PRAGMAs. 3. Instantiate collector = HistoryCollector(repo).
integration_logic.message_flow: 1. App layer (bot.py) validates the source against ACLs. 2. For both incoming updates and outgoing (bot) messages, call collector.process_message(message). 3. Collector extracts metadata via private _extract_* methods. 4. Collector handles recursive UPSERT for 'reply_to_message' to ensure referential integrity for reply chains. 5. Collector calls repository methods to persist data using (message_id, chat_id) as the unique identifier.

operational_notes.note: Identity Mapping: The system avoids internal surrogate IDs for messages. All relations are built upon (message_id, chat_id) pairs to maintain direct parity with Telegram Bot API state.
Connection Safety: Every repository method opens and closes its own connection via a private _get_connection() helper, ensuring thread safety in a multi-handler environment.
WAL Mode: Enabled to support concurrent read operations by maintenance tools (dump_db.py) while the bot processes updates.
